title: Goldfussia yunnanensis
taxonomy: Goldfussia yunnanensis is classified within the family Gesneriaceae. This family comprises a diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the 'African violet' or 'gesneriad' family. The genus Goldfussia is a part of this family and primarily consists of flowering plants native to Southeast Asia. Goldfussia yunnanensis is native to Yunnan in China. The exact classification of Goldfussia yunnanensis also includes its species rank within the genus.

intro: Goldfussia yunnanensis, also known as Yunnan goldfussia, is a plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and aromatic leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to aid in digestion, relieve anxiety and stress, and promote relaxation, due to its purported health benefits. Bioactive constituents of Goldfussia yunnanensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being added to traditional Chinese medicine formulas to promote digestive health and alleviate stress.
